extracted_codes.context: Case Possible ICD-9 code for atrial fibrillation

extracted_codes.display: Atrial Flutter
extracted_codes.context: Case Possible ICD-9 code for atrial flutter

clinical_criteria: Record contains at least one ECG (without AF keywords) for control designation
Subcase 1: NOT (ECG Impression = Myocardial infarction keywords within 14 days pre or post)
Subcase 2: ECG Impression = Myocardial infarction keywords within 14 days (pre or post) OR CPT/ICD 9 codes = cardiac surgery
All Cases: NOT heart transplant (CPT/ICD 9 codes)
Controls: Record contains none of the Case keywords or ICD-9 codes for atrial fibrillation/flutter
Controls: No keywords for Atrial flutter, Atrial tachycardia
algorithm_summary: This phenotype identifies atrial fibrillation (AF) through ECG impressions matching AF keywords (using REGEXP pattern for 'AF', 'A* fib*', 'afib', 'aflutter', etc.) or through ICD-9 codes 427.31/427.32. Cases are categorized into two subcases: Subcase 1 excludes patients with myocardial infarction within 14 days or cardiac surgery; Subcase 2 includes patients with MI within 14 days or cardiac surgery. All cases exclude heart transplant patients. Case Possible designation applies to patients with AF/flutter keywords in narrative notes or ICD-9 codes. Controls have no AF/flutter codes, no AF keywords in any notes, but must have at least one ECG without AF findings.
